title: salix × rubella
intro: Salix × rubella, also known as the weeping willow hybrid, is a cross between the white willow (Salix alba) and the goosely willow (Salix viminalis), resulting in a versatile and adaptable plant species. The bark and leaves of Salix × rubella have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving compounds that are similar to those found in aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × rubella include reducing fever, alleviating headaches, and soothing menstrual cramps, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health issues. The herbal preparations of Salix × rubella often involve infusing the bark or leaves in hot water to create a tea or tincture that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
